pages.raw_text:
To the Editor of the Press: The
present trouble between the rest
aurant keepers and their employees
being Of some interest to the pub
lie, l would like to present our side
Of the question. Several years ago
the bosses put in their first kick
against raising the girls wages.
but a few months ago they went
after the miscellaneous help and
wanted us to throw them out of the
union. and said they would never
recognize them as union men. We
refused their offers and ignored
their threats, and the dishwashers
won, though we have never been
able to protect them without an
open rupture. Now this last de-
feat seems to hurt them. A dish
washer gets SLAP per day of twelve
hours. seven days a week. Is that
unreasonable? or course, he is
Overworked and underpaid, and
doesn't last long, generally speak
ing; and If he takes layoff he
is called a loafer and a hobo.


ne OOSSeS lOTCeu tnelr contract
on us and gave us but a short time
to consider it. Almost every as
ISOLATION house began to hire their
help from the employment offices.
where men had to pay big fees for
jobs they could not fill. Their con
tract reads that all help must come
through the union office when pos
sible. They have hung up rules on
the walls that are an insult to
every employee. They have ig-
nOred us on every occasion and
have violated their contract by
working our girls and boys twelve
hours instead of ten and eleven, as
per contract.


When they opened their employ.
